Container.Name: Unraid Monitor Bot
Container.Overview:
Telegram bot for monitoring Docker containers and Unraid servers. Get real-time alerts, check container status, view logs, control containers, and monitor server health - all from Telegram.
On first launch, an interactive setup wizard in Telegram guides you through connecting to your Unraid server and auto-classifies your containers into categories (priority, protected, watched, killable, ignored). Re-run anytime with /setup.
Features: - Interactive setup wizard with container auto-classification - Container crash alerts with quick action buttons - Resource monitoring (CPU/memory thresholds) - Log watching with error detection - AI-powered diagnostics and container classification (works with Anthropic, OpenAI, or Ollama) - Unraid server monitoring (temps, array, disks) - Natural language chat interface - Smart ignore patterns for known errors - Mute system for alerts
Setup requires a Telegram bot token from @BotFather and your Telegram user ID. After installing, message your bot with /start to begin the setup wizard.
Container.Description: Telegram bot for monitoring Docker containers and Unraid servers with AI-powered diagnostics and interactive setup wizard.

Config.Name: Allowed Telegram Users
Config.Description: Comma-separated list of Telegram user IDs allowed to use the bot (get yours from @userinfobot)

Config.Name: Anthropic API Key
Config.Description: Optional: Enables AI features via Anthropic Claude models

Config.Name: OpenAI API Key
Config.Description: Optional: Enables AI features via OpenAI models

Config.Name: Ollama Host
Config.Description: Optional: Ollama server URL for local LLM support

Config.Name: Default Model
Config.Description: Optional: Override default AI model (e.g. qwen2.5:7b, gpt-4o)

Config.Name: Unraid API Key
Config.Description: Optional: Enables Unraid server monitoring (get from Unraid Connect plugin)

Config.Name: PUID
Config.Description: User ID for file ownership (99 = nobody on Unraid)

Config.Name: PGID
Config.Description: Group ID for file ownership (100 = users on Unraid)
